title: Anatomy of the Prostate
notesHtml:
Anatomy of the Prostate

The prostate is a walnut-sized gland located below the bladder. It consists of peripheral, central, and transition zones described by McNeal.
plainText:
Anatomy of the Prostate
The prostate is a walnut-sized gland located below the bladder. It consists of peripheral, central, and transition zones described by McNeal.

title: BPH Pathophysiology
notesHtml:
BPH Pathophysiology

Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) involves both static (tissue enlargement) and dynamic (smooth muscle tone) components. The transition zone is the primary site of BPH development.
plainText:
BPH Pathophysiology
Benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) involves both static (tissue enlargement) and dynamic (smooth muscle tone) components. The transition zone is the primary site of BPH development.
